      "assessment_status": "formative"
    },

  ],
  "practicals": [
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class IX History",
      "source_text": "French Revolution: Outline political map of France. Locate/label/ identify Bordeaux, Nantes, Paris and Marseille; Socialism in Europe... Major countries of First World War... Nazism and the Rise of Hitler... Axis Powers...",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class IX Geography",
      "source_text": "India: Size and location (States, Capitals, Tropic of Cancer, Standard Meridian); Physical features (Mountain Ranges, Peaks, Plateaus, Coastal Plains); Drainage system (Rivers, Lakes); Climate; Population density.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class X History",
      "source_text": "Nationalism in India: Congress sessions (1920 Calcutta, 1920 Nagpur, 1927 Madras); Satyagraha movements; Jallianwala Bagh; Dandi March.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class X Geography",
      "source_text": "Resources and Development (Soil Types); Water Resources (Dams: Salal, Bhakra Nangal, etc.); Agriculture (Rice/Wheat areas, Producer states); Minerals and Energy Resources (Iron Ore, Coal, Oil Fields, Power Plants); Manufacturing Industries; Lifelines of National Economy (Sea Ports, Airports).",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "activities": [
    {
      "title": "Multiple Assessment Activities - Class IX/X",
      "source_text": "Quiz, debate, role play, viva-voce, group discussion, visual expression, interactive bulletin boards, gallery walks, exit cards, concept maps, peer assessment, self-assessment.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Subject Enrichment Activity - Class IX",
      "source_text": "Project work (Interdisciplinary)-Disaster Management. Various forms of art must be integrated in the project work.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Portfolio Activities",
      "source_text": "Classroom, work done (activities/assignments) reflections, narrations, journals etc. Achievements of the student in the subject throughout the year.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "projects": [
    {
      "title": "Class IX Interdisciplinary Project: Disaster Management",
      "source_text": "Every student must undertake one project on Disaster Management. Objectives include creating awareness, preparing students to face situations, ensuring participation in risk reduction plans.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Class IX Interdisciplinary Project: Forest Society and Colonialism / Natural Vegetation",
      "source_text": "Interdisciplinary project with Chapter 5 of Geography 'Natural Vegetation and Wildlife'. Compare forest situations in pre-colonial, colonial and post-colonial era. Devise ways to protect forest vegetation.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Class X Interdisciplinary Project: Consumer Awareness / Social Issues / Sustainable Development",
      "source_text": "Every student must undertake one project on Consumer Awareness OR Social Issues OR Sustainable Development. Apply Social Science concepts.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Class X Interdisciplinary Project: Globalisation and Lifelines",
      "source_text": "Interdisciplinary project with History Chapter III 'The Making of a Global World', Geography Chapter 7 'Lifelines of National Economy' and Economics Chapter 4 'Globalisation'. Analyse implications of globalisation.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "assessment_information": {
    "marks_distribution": [
      "Class IX History: 20 Marks (18+2 map pointing)",
      "Class IX Geography: 20 Marks (17+3 map pointing)",
      "Class IX Political Science: 20 Marks",
      "Class IX Economics: 20 Marks",
      "Class X History: 20 Marks (18+2 map pointing)",
      "Class X Geography: 20 Marks (17+3 map pointing)",
      "Class X Political Science: 20 Marks",
      "Class X Economics: 20 Marks",
      "Internal Assessment (Both Classes): 20 Marks total (Periodic Test 5, Multiple Assessment 5, Subject Enrichment/Project Work 5, Portfolio 5)",
      "Board Exam Question Paper Design Class X: Total 80 Marks. MCQs (1 Mark) - 20; Long Answer (2 Marks) - 8; Long Answer (3 Marks) - 15; Case Study (4 Marks) - 12; Long Answer (5 Marks) - 20; Map Pointing - 5."
    ],
    "excluded_or_non_assessed_content": [
      "Chapter 5 Pastoralists in the Modern World (Class IX History): Assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only.",
      "Chapter 1 The Story of Village Palampur (Class IX Economics): To be assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only.",
      "Natural Vegetation and Wildlife map pointing (Class IX Geography): Only map pointing to be evaluated in the annual examination.",
      "The Age of Industrialisation (Class X History): To be assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only.",
      "Lifelines of National Economy map pointing (Class X Geography): Only map pointing to be evaluated in the Board Examination."
    ],
    "teacher_notes": [
      "Refer to NCF-2023 pages 320-323 for Aims & Objectives.",
      "Interdisciplinary projects must be done at school only; carryover of project to home is strictly avoided.",
      "Projects and models should be made from eco-friendly products without incurring too much expenditure.",
      "Records pertaining to internal assessment (projects) will be maintained for a period of three months from the date of declaration of result for verification.",
      "Maps available on the website of Govt. of India may be used.",
      "Project work can culminate in various forms: PowerPoint Presentation, Exhibition, Skit, albums/files/song and dance or culture show/story telling/debate/panel discussion, paper presentation suitable to Visually Impaired Candidates."
    ]
  }
}
